description.en: A golden, deep-fried yeast bun from South African kitchens, crisp outside and fluffy within. Served hot, split open and stuffed with curried mince, or simply with jam, honey or syrup for a sweeter version; a fixture of township and farm-stall cooking alike.
description.de: Ein goldener, frittierter Hefeball aus südafrikanischen Küchen, außen knusprig und innen locker. Heiß serviert, aufgeschnitten und mit Curry-Hackfleisch gefüllt, oder einfach mit Marmelade, Honig oder Sirup für eine süßere Variante; fester Bestandteil der Township- und Farmküche.
description.es: Un bollo de levadura dorado y frito de las cocinas sudafricanas, crujiente por fuera y esponjoso por dentro. Se sirve caliente, relleno de carne picada al curry, o con mermelada y miel en su versión dulce, un clásico de townships y granjas.
description.fr: Une boule de pâte levée dorée et frite des cuisines sud-africaines, croustillante dehors et moelleuse dedans. Servie chaude, fourrée de viande hachée au curry, ou avec confiture et miel en version sucrée, un classique des townships et des fermes.

origin.note: Called vetkoek ('fat cake') in Afrikaans and amagwinya or magwinya in Nguni and Sotho languages; a township and farm-kitchen staple eaten sweet or split and filled with curried mince.

ingredients.note: absorbed during frying; the deep-frying oil bath itself is mostly reused, not counted

keeping.note: Best eaten hot the day it is fried; reheat in a hot oven to recrisp rather than microwaving, which turns them soft and greasy.

summary.en: Combine salt, sugar, yeast and lukewarm water; let stand about 5 minutes until it foams.
Stir in the flour until just combined into a shaggy dough.
Cover loosely and let rise in a warm place 1 to 2 hours, until doubled.
Knead the risen dough until firm, adding a little flour as needed, then roll to about 2.5 cm thick and cut into rounds.
Fry in oil at about 177°C until deep golden on both sides, 3 to 5 minutes depending on size.
Lift out with a slotted spoon, drain on paper towels, and serve hot, split and filled with curried mince or spread with jam.

source.adaptation: Frying oil absorbed into the dough estimated at 10% of the dough's weight, since the deep-frying bath itself is mostly reused, not consumed.
